turns.text: Hey Ben, do you have any plans for Saturday?
Not yet. I was thinking about staying home, but that sounds boring.
Well, I want to arrange a picnic in the park. Do you want to come?
That sounds great! What do we need to prepare?
I'll make some sandwiches. Can you bring drinks and a blanket?
Sure. Do you prefer the park near the river or the one by the library?
I prefer the one near the river. There are big trees, and it's really comfortable to sit under them.
Good idea. I like that park too. It's less crowded on weekends.
Perfect. Let's meet at ten in the morning. I'll prepare everything the night before.
Sounds like a plan. I'll arrange to pick you up on the way.
Thanks! It'll be a comfortable and relaxing day.
I can't wait. See you Saturday!
Sophie, I got a job offer from a big company downtown!
That's amazing! What kind of career path does it offer?
It's in marketing. The salary is much better than what I earn now.
How much more would you earn?
About thirty percent more. And there's a chance for a promotion after one year.
That's a big step for your career. Do you prefer this company over your current one?
I think so. The office looks comfortable and modern.
You should prepare for the move soon. When do they need your answer?
By Friday. I need to arrange a meeting with my current boss first.
Good idea. Prepare what you want to say so it goes smoothly.
A higher salary and a promotion path — it's exactly what I need right now.
I'm happy for you. Go arrange everything and start this new chapter!
Jack, have you seen all the rubbish in the park? The pollution is terrible.
I know. We need to take care of our environment.
I want to start a clean-up group. We could recycle the bottles and cans we find.
Great idea. But we'd need some money for bags and gloves. Can we earn it somehow?
Maybe the city will help. Protecting natural spaces is part of their job.
True. I heard they have a salary for people who work on environmental projects.
Not a full salary, but they give small payments. It could help with costs.
We should also teach people to recycle properly. Most pollution comes from laziness.
Exactly. If we keep the park natural and clean, more people will enjoy it.
This could even help my career. I want to work in environmental science.
That's wonderful. Maybe this project will lead to a promotion of some kind for your studies.
I hope so. Let's start by cleaning the river path and recycling everything we find.
Mark, I found a great recipe for pasta online. Want to try it tonight?
Sure! What ingredients do we need?
Tomatoes, garlic, cheese, and fresh herbs. The flavour should be amazing.
I'll go to the shop. Should I use my own bags? I want to help the environment.
Yes, please. We should recycle the old ones too — don't throw them away.
Of course. I also want to buy some fruit for dessert.
Good idea. Get some strawberries — they have a natural sweetness that's perfect.
Love it. So the recipe needs fresh ingredients for the best flavour?
Exactly. Canned tomatoes don't taste the same. Fresh ones have more natural flavour.
I agree. Less pollution from packaging too, if we buy loose vegetables.
That's true! And for dessert, I'll make chocolate mousse. The recipe looks simple.
Pasta and dessert — sounds like a perfect evening. Let me get the ingredients now.
David, did you meet the new woman who moved in next door?
Not really. She's still just an acquaintance — we only said hello once.
Her name is Sarah. I want to introduce you properly.
That would be nice. Should we invite her over for dinner?
Great idea! I have a recipe for chicken that everyone loves.
What ingredients do you need? I'll go shopping.
Just chicken, lemon, and herbs. The flavour is simple but really good.
Perfect. And maybe a dessert too? We want to make a good first impression.
Definitely. A nice dinner is a great way to bond with someone new.
I agree. An acquaintance can become a true friend if you make the effort.
Let's invite her for Friday. I'll introduce you both and we'll have a lovely evening.
Sounds wonderful. Building a bond with neighbours is important.
James, I've been looking at holiday destinations. How about Greece?
Greece sounds amazing. Is there a direct flight from here?
Yes, about three hours. The journey isn't long at all.
Great. Should we invite anyone else? Maybe your friend Laura?
She's just an acquaintance really. I'd prefer it to be just us.
Fair enough. When should we go? I need to book the flight early.
June would be perfect. I'll introduce you to a great travel website.
Thanks. I need to buy new luggage too — my old bag is broken.
There's a sale at the mall. We should get luggage that's light and easy to carry.
Good thinking. The destination has beaches and old towns, so we'll need different clothes.
The bond we'll build on this journey will be amazing. Just us and the sea.
I can't wait. Let me book the flight tonight!
Alex, I went to the doctor. She said I need to change my diet.
What did she recommend?
More vegetables, less sugar. And I need regular exercise.
I started running every morning. It's become a habit now.
That's great. I want to build a good habit like that too.
Start small. Even twenty minutes of exercise helps a lot.
She also said stress is bad. I was so tired after our journey last month.
Travel can be hard. Remember how heavy your luggage was at the airport?
Yes! I could barely lift it. Next time I'll pack lighter for the flight.
I recommend walking as exercise. Pick a nice destination nearby and walk there.
Good idea. A healthy diet and walking would change everything for me.
It really would. Start this week — make exercise a daily habit.
Ryan, I just got a new tablet. Can you help me set up this device?
Sure! First, create a strong password so your device stays safe.
What kind of password should I use?
Mix numbers and letters. Then check for a software update — they fix problems.
Okay. I also want to download some fitness apps. The doctor says I need more exercise.
Good idea! I recommend this one — it tracks your diet and exercise together.
Perfect. I'm trying to make healthy eating a habit.
The app sends reminders, so it helps you build the habit.
I'll download it right now. Is the update finished yet?
Almost. The device needs a few more minutes.
While we wait, can you recommend any other apps to download?
Try a language app. Set a strong password for each account and keep your device updated.
Emma, the football tournament starts next week. My brother is playing!
That's exciting! Is he a good athlete?
He's one of the best. Their coach has been training them hard.
What's their goal for the tournament?
To reach the final. The coach thinks they have a real chance.
I'd love to watch. Can I download the schedule on my device?
Yes, the tournament has its own app. Just check for the latest update first.
Good, I'll set a password and log in. Who are they playing first?
The city team from the north. My brother, the athlete, says they're tough.
Every athlete wants to win. The goal is always the same — play your best.
True. The coach told them to focus and stay sharp.
I'll download the app now and make sure I don't miss any tournament games!
Tom, I have to give a speech at work tomorrow and I'm so nervous.
I understand that emotion. Public speaking can be really scary.
I don't know how to express my ideas clearly in front of everyone.
Try to stay calm. Take a deep breath before you begin.
What if my mind goes blank? I need more courage.
Think of it like an athlete before a tournament. They're nervous too, but they focus.
That's a good way to look at it. An athlete has a coach to help them.
And you have me! Your goal is simple — share what you know.
The emotion I feel is mostly fear. But I'll try to stay calm.
Good. Express your points one at a time and don't rush.
You're right. I need to find the courage to just start talking.
You'll do great. Even the best athletes feel nervous before the tournament starts.
Mark, I'm worried about my history assignment. It's due on Friday.
Do you attend all the classes? That usually helps.
I attend every one, but the knowledge needed for this paper is really deep.
Don't let the emotion of stress take over. Stay calm and work through it.
I'm trying, but I don't want to fail. This assignment is worth a lot of marks.
You won't fail if you keep at it. Have the courage to ask the teacher for help.
That's true. I should express my problems to her instead of hiding them.
Exactly. Courage means asking for help when you need it.
The assignment asks us to show deep knowledge of three different time periods.
Start with the one you know best. Stay calm and build from there.
Good advice. I'll attend the study group tomorrow too.
Smart. Express your ideas clearly in the assignment and you won't fail.
James, I heard you joined a band! Are you going to perform at the festival?
Yes! We have a rehearsal tonight. Our singer likes to compose her own songs.
She composes her own music? That takes a lot of knowledge.
It does. She studied music at college and still attends writing workshops.
How big is the audience at the festival?
About five hundred people. A big audience makes me nervous, but I'm excited.
You'll be fine. Think of the rehearsal as your practice assignment.
Good point. If I prepare well, I won't fail on stage.
Exactly. When do you attend the next rehearsal?
Tomorrow evening. We need to perform five songs and compose a new ending for one.
The audience will love it. Use your knowledge of music to give a great show.
Thanks, Mia. I don't want to fail — I'll practise hard at every rehearsal.
Ben, I have so many things to do today. The laundry is piling up.
Same here. I need to run some errands before the shops close.
What errands do you have?
I need to go to the grocery store and return a book to the library.
I hate doing chores on the weekend, but they have to get done.
True. My sister has a rehearsal for her dance show today. She composes the moves herself.
Wow, that's cool. Does she perform in front of a big audience?
Yes, about two hundred people come to watch her perform.
Amazing. Meanwhile, I'm here doing laundry and grocery shopping!
That's life. Let's finish the chores quickly — I have three errands left.
I'll do the laundry first, then go to the grocery store.
Good plan. After the errands, we can relax. Even the audience at a show gets to sit down!
David, I'm thinking about opening a small bakery. How do I get customers?
Quality is the key. If the bread is good, customers will come back.
That makes sense. I also need to think about delivery for people who can't come in.
Good idea. Did you find a deal on a shop space?
Yes, there's a place near the grocery store on Green Street. The rent is a good deal.
Nice. Running a bakery has a lot of daily chores though — baking, cleaning, ordering.
I know. I'll need to do laundry for the aprons every day too.
And errands — picking up flour, butter, all the supplies.
True. But if the quality stays high, I'll build a loyal group of customers.
Delivery will help too. Some customers want fresh bread at their door.
I've already planned the delivery area. The grocery store owner might send people my way too.
That's a great deal for both of you. Just be ready for all the errands and chores that come with it!
Alex, the science fair is next week. Have you finished your research?
Almost. I did an experiment about how plants grow in different soils.
What did you discover?
Plants in dark soil grew twice as fast. My research showed the quality of soil matters most.
Can you predict which plant will be tallest by the fair?
I think the one by the window. I can predict it'll grow three more centimetres.
That's great. My experiment is about water — I want to discover if cold water helps flowers last longer.
Interesting! The customer at the flower shop near school might know about that.
Maybe. She said there's a deal on fresh flowers if I need more for testing.
Nice. Does the delivery come quickly? You need them fresh for the experiment.
Same-day delivery. The quality of the flowers has been really good so far.
Let's both do our best. I'll predict the results tonight and finish my research.
Sophie, I got a parking ticket yesterday. I don't think it's fair.
What happened? Were you parked in the wrong place?
No. The sign was hidden by a tree. There's no clear evidence I could see it.
You should fight it. Do you need a lawyer?
Maybe. I did some research online and discovered that others had the same problem.
That's useful evidence. If many people made the same mistake, the sign is the problem.
Exactly. I want to go to court and explain what happened.
A good lawyer can help in court. I'd predict the judge will agree with you.
I hope so. My friend is a lawyer and she said it sounds like a simple experiment in fairness.
An experiment in fairness — that's one way to put it! The evidence is clear though.
She discovered that most people win these cases when the sign isn't visible.
Then it should be fair in the end. Take your evidence to court and let the lawyer handle it.
Jack, look at all this fresh food at the farmers' market!
This farmer says his crops are all organic. No chemicals at all.
That's great. They harvest the vegetables early in the morning for the best taste.
I want to try this grain bread. It's made from wheat, oats, and rye.
Looks delicious. My neighbour's a lawyer and she buys everything organic here.
Really? Well, the evidence shows organic food is better for you.
True. She says it's only fair that farmers get good prices for quality crops.
I agree. They work so hard to harvest everything by hand.
This grain bread is amazing. We should buy some for the week.
And some organic tomatoes. The crops here look so much fresher than the supermarket.
The court of public opinion says fresh is best! Let's get a bag of everything.
Ha! Fair point. These organic crops are worth every penny.
Ryan, the community centre needs volunteers for the food bank. Want to help?
I'd love to volunteer. What do they do there?
They collect food and give it to families living in poverty.
That's important. You can also donate money, right?
Yes. The charity accepts food, money, and clothes.
I'll donate some canned food. But fresh organic food would be better for them.
The charity works with local farmers. They harvest extra crops and give them to the centre.
That's wonderful. Grain and vegetables straight from the farm.
Exactly. Poverty is a big problem here, but people are helping.
How often do volunteers work at the food bank?
Most come once a week. Even a few hours helps. You can donate time or food.
Count me in. Helping fight poverty through this charity is something I really want to do.
Mark, did you vote today? It's election day!
Yes, I voted this morning. Which candidate did you choose?
The one with the strongest policy on education and health.
Her campaign has been really good. She talks about real problems.
She wants to help charities that fight poverty. That matters to me.
Me too. She also said she'd support volunteers in schools and hospitals.
The other candidate doesn't have a clear policy on those issues.
His campaign focuses mostly on business. Not bad, but not enough.
I think people should donate their time to help during elections — volunteer at the polling station.
Good point. Fighting poverty and helping communities is what we should vote for.
Exactly. Every vote counts. The right candidate with the right policy can change things.
I hope the results show that people care about charities, poverty, and real change.
James, have you seen this new app? It helps you find local events on your smartphone.
No, tell me more. What's the app called?
CityLife. You can find it on their website or in the app store.
Let me open the browser and look. Is it free?
Yes, completely free. You just sign up with your email.
Nice. What kind of events does the app show on the website?
Markets, concerts, sports — everything. One candidate even uses it for campaign events.
Really? So you can follow a candidate's campaign right on your smartphone?
Yes! And there's a section where you can vote for your favourite local events.
That's clever. Any policy news or just fun things?
Mostly fun, but they share local policy updates too. Open the browser and try it.
I'm downloading the app now. My smartphone needed something like this. Great website too!
